result:
If I were to change the world in one way, I would implement **universal radical empathy.**

I don’t mean a passive form of politeness, but a hard-wired, cognitive ability for every person to truly experience the internal reality of another. 

If you could feel the physical hunger of a child you’ve never met, the genuine terror of someone fleeing violence, or the crushing weight of systemic hopelessness felt by those on the margins, the world’s current mechanics would be impossible to maintain. Greed, indifference, and cruelty depend entirely on the "othering" of people—the mental firewall that allows us to view someone else's suffering as something happening to an object, rather than a feeling subject.

If that barrier were dismantled, conflict would become a form of self-harm. Exploitation would become a visceral, shared pain. We would be forced into a state of hyper-cooperation not because of laws or morality, but because we would no longer be able to distinguish our own well-being from the well-being of the collective.

It would be a chaotic, overwhelming, and transformative shift. It would likely end the "individualistic" era of humanity, but it would almost certainly end our greatest source of self-inflicted misery.
